words.definition: people who work in farming and growing crops
in an organized and methodical way
extreme measures taken to solve a serious problem
moved back to a safe place
people or animals who do bad or harmful things
to spend time with or be connected to someone
free from fault or responsibility for wrongdoing
words.sentence: From The Price of Bad Company

words.definition: connections or relationships with other people
words.sentence: From The Price of Bad Company

questions.question: What made this farmer successful?
options: He was lucky with the weather
His hard work, knowledge, and meticulous care
He had the biggest farm

questions.question: What was the farmer most proud of?
options: His house and barn
His grain fields with fine wheat and corn
His collection of farming tools

questions.question: What problem did the farmer discover?
options: His crops were not growing well
A flock of cranes was eating his grain
There was too much rain

questions.question: How did the cranes behave in the farmer's fields?
options: They were careful not to damage anything
They showed no respect and wasted grain carelessly
They only ate a tiny amount

questions.question: What solution did the farmer decide to try?
options: Building a fence around his crops
Setting a large net trap to capture the cranes
Moving his crops to a different field

questions.question: How did the farmer prepare his trap?
options: He worked quickly without much care
He worked carefully by lamplight and scattered extra grain as bait
He asked his neighbors to help him

questions.question: What was the stork looking for in the field?
options: The farmer's valuable grain
Insects, frogs, and small creatures - her natural food
A place to build a nest

questions.question: How was the stork different from the cranes?
options: She was larger and stronger
She was kind, honest, and respectful of human property
She was more colorful

questions.question: How did the stork try to defend herself?
options: She tried to escape from the trap
She explained that she only ate insects and never stole grain
She blamed the cranes for everything

questions.question: Why didn't the farmer believe the stork?
options: He knew she was lying
His anger made him unforgiving and he judged her by the company she kept
He was too busy to listen

questions.question: What lesson does the stork's situation teach us?
options: That storks should never fly with cranes
To be careful about the company we keep because others may judge us by it
That farmers are always unfair

questions.question: What does this story remind us about the world?
options: That everything always works out perfectly
That the world is not always fair and good people sometimes suffer
That all birds are the same

informationalTexts.title: A Hardworking Farmer
informationalTexts.content:
Learning about A Hardworking Farmer is interesting and important for second graders. There are many fascinating facts about A Hardworking Farmer that help us understand the world better. By studying different topics, students build knowledge and develop curiosity.

When we explore A Hardworking Farmer, we make connections to other subjects and our daily lives. Teachers and books can help us learn more about A Hardworking Farmer. The more we learn, the better we understand how things work and why they matter.

questions.question: What can you learn about A Hardworking Farmer?
options: nothing useful
fascinating facts that help us understand the world
only boring things

questions.question: Why is it good to learn about A Hardworking Farmer?
options: it helps us understand the world better
it's not important
only adults need to know

grammarLessons.content:
**Nouns**

This lesson focuses on Nouns. As you read today's story, pay attention to examples of this concept.

**Key Points:**
• Understand the concept
• Find examples in the story
• Practice using it correctly

**Practice:**
Apply what you learned by completing the exercises.

**Remember:** Nouns is an important language skill!
grammarLessons.objective: Students will understand and apply Nouns.

languageLessons.content:
**ABC Order**

This language lesson teaches ABC Order. Look for examples in today's story.

**Key Ideas:**
• Learn the concept
• Find examples
• Practice the skill

**Practice Activities:**
Complete the exercises to master this skill.

**Remember:** Practice makes perfect!
languageLessons.objective: Students will learn about ABC Order.

languageLessons.content:
Rhyming words sound the SAME at the end. They make poems and stories fun to read aloud!

**Rhyming Words:**
• cat, hat, mat, sat (all end with -at)
• play, day, say, way (all end with -ay)
• book, look, cook, took (all end with -ook)

**From today's story:**
Listen for words that rhyme as you read!

**Practice - Find the rhyme:**
1. Which word rhymes with "dog"? (log, big, cat)
2. Which word rhymes with "jump"? (run, bump, sit)
3. Which word rhymes with "bed"? (chair, red, sleep)

**Remember:** Listen to the END of words to find rhymes!
languageLessons.objective: Students will identify and create rhyming words.
